rationale: Builds on the current best script by keeping the existing soft quant-aware ramp and quantizer, but splitting Muon updates so int8 projection matrices get an extra LR damp only during the quant-aware tail. The hypothesis is that early projection weights are the most quantization-sensitive remaining matrices, so letting qkv/fc weights keep the current tail LR while slowing projection drift should improve the final int8 roundtrip score without changing artifact format or spending bytes on more float rescue.
title: Quant-Aware Projection Muon LR Split
